# User Presence

> Track and broadcast user presence status (online, away, busy, offline) with automatic detection based on connection and activity

**Category:** Communication · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** presence · status · real-time · availability

## What this does

Track and broadcast user presence status (online, away, busy, offline) with automatic detection based on connection and activity

Specifies 8 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **user_id** *(text, required)* — User ID
- **status** *(select, required)* — Presence Status
- **status_text** *(text, optional)* — Status Message
- **connection_id** *(text, optional)* — Connection ID
- **node_id** *(text, optional)* — Node ID
- **last_login** *(datetime, optional)* — Last Login

## What must be true

- **general:** Each user connection is tracked independently; overall presence is derived from all active connections, When a user's last connection is removed, their presence transitions to offline automatically, Users may manually override auto-detected presence with online, away, or busy, Status text is user-controlled and persists independently of the connection state, An invisible or disabled status may be allowed or blocked by system configuration, Rate limiting must be applied to manual status changes to prevent abuse, Presence updates must be broadcast to all subscribers in real time, Stale connections on a node must be cleaned up when that node goes offline, Custom status names can be defined system-wide; each maps to one of the base status types, Status text changes require that the system allows user status message modifications

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Connection Established** — when user authenticates and opens a connection; This is a new connection, then Connection is registered and user status is set to online.
- **Status Set Manually** — when user submits a status change request; status in ["online","away","busy","offline"], then User status is updated and broadcast to all subscribers.
- **Status Text Updated** — when user submits a status text change; status_text exists, then Status message is persisted and broadcast.
- **Connection Removed** — when a user connection is closed or timed out; connection_id exists, then Connection removed; if no connections remain, user transitions to offline.
- **Last Connection Removed** — when last active connection for the user is closed, then User status changes to offline and subscribers are notified.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Invisible Status Blocked** — when user requests offline/invisible status; allow_invisible eq false, then Status change rejected because invisible mode is disabled by system configuration. *(error: `PRESENCE_INVISIBLE_NOT_ALLOWED`)*
- **Status Change Rate Limited** — when user exceeds allowed rate of manual status changes, then Request rejected due to rate limiting; user must wait before changing status again. *(error: `PRESENCE_RATE_LIMITED`)*
- **Status Text Change Not Allowed** — when user attempts to change status text; allow_status_message_change eq false, then Status text change rejected because the feature is disabled by system configuration. *(error: `PRESENCE_STATUS_TEXT_NOT_ALLOWED`)*

## Errors it can return

- `PRESENCE_INVISIBLE_NOT_ALLOWED` — Invisible status is not available on this server
- `PRESENCE_RATE_LIMITED` — Too many status changes. Please wait before trying again
- `PRESENCE_STATUS_TEXT_NOT_ALLOWED` — Status message changes are not allowed on this server

## Connects to

- **user-profile** *(required)* — User identity is required to associate presence with an account
- **typing-indicators** *(recommended)* — Typing indicators complement presence by showing in-room activity
- **read-receipts** *(optional)* — Read receipts may use last-seen timestamps that relate to presence
